name: Puerto Deportivo de Ayamonte
approachNotes: Spanish bank of the Rio Guadiana at the PT/ES border. Guadiana bar shallow/variable - enter on second half of a rising tide. Entrance can fall to 2-3 m at MLW; bar dredged ~2016 but sandbanks shift after floods. Keep to the Portuguese (W) bank until well into the estuary, then turn to the Spanish side. Strong cross-current on the ebb; driftwood hazard. Port of Entry - Customs available.
nearestTown: Ayamonte centre 10 min walk; full provisioning; ferry to Portugal; Faro airport ~60 km.
notes: Southernmost western Gulf-of-Cadiz stop; good refuge and Atlantic-voyage start. Blue Flag.
orcaNotes: Gulf of Cadiz outer waters have had orca interactions in season (GTOA); risk rises markedly approaching the Strait.

name: Marina Isla Canela
approachNotes: In the Carreras River (Cano de Isla Canela), ~650 m upriver of Punta del Caiman. Access channel min 50 m wide, min 3 m depth. Tidal streams up to 2 kt; enter on rising tide. Chart D-440.
nearestTown: Ayamonte ~6 km; Isla Canela resort adjacent; Faro airport ~60 km.
notes: Resort marina; quieter alternative to Ayamonte. Blue Flag.

name: Puerto Deportivo de Isla Cristina
approachNotes: Enter Rio Carreras channel on ~312. Red/white landfall buoy half a mile offshore - leave close to starboard. Lit red/green mole-end markers. Keep to port side of buoyed channel to avoid shallows to starboard. Entrance ~3.0 m, marina ~2.5 m. Currents up to 2 kt on ebb; rising tide. Fishing/commercial traffic.
nearestTown: Isla Cristina (~500 m); excellent fish market; Faro ~70 km / Huelva ~60 km.
notes: Busy fishing port with sports marina. Travel lift 32 t, crane 5 t, hardstanding. Workmanlike haulout stop.

name: Marina El Rompido (Rio Piedras)
approachNotes: Rio Piedras, 16 nm E of PT border. Landfall buoy ~37 11.58N 007 03.16W; river entrance buoyed. Bar reduces to ~1 m at MLW; HW depths 3.0-4.7 m. DO NOT attempt near LW or in onshore wind/swell. Rising tide near HW essential. Shifting sandbanks; buoys may lag after heavy weather. Marina ~6 nm upriver (dunes/wildlife). Staff assist mooring.
nearestTown: El Rompido village adjacent; Cartaya ~8 km; Faro airport ~80 km.
notes: Scenic tidal-river stop; shallow bar -> shoal-draft or HW only. 24h security, supermarket adjacent.
verifyNeeded: bar depth current survey - chart cross-check essential

name: Puerto de Punta Umbria (Marina Umbria / RCMT)
approachNotes: Rio de Punta Umbria (Odiel tributary). Long mole with S cardinal at end protects W side of estuary. Channel buoyed from entrance. Two marinas: RCMT (~197 berths, <=12 m) ~1 nm in; Marina Umbria (~260 berths, 65 transit) a further 0.75 nm upriver. Entry near HW advised. Chart D-440.
nearestTown: Punta Umbria resort adjacent; Huelva ~20 km; Seville airport ~90 km.
notes: Popular local weekend marina; access to Donana NP. Berth count is aggregate; transit berths at Marina Umbria.

name: Puerto Deportivo de Mazagon
approachNotes: At the Rio Odiel/Huelva entrance. Curving 3-nm mole extends the S bank ~1.5 nm seaward; prominent white pillar with red top at end. Head for the W cardinal buoy at the start of the buoyed channel; markers lead ~1 nm N to the marina. Training wall protects from S. Entrance ~4.5 m, marina 2.5-4.5 m. Well protected all directions.
nearestTown: Mazagon village ~1 km; Huelva ~23 km; Seville airport ~80 km.
notes: Excellent well-protected marina; good for extended stay/maintenance. Travel lift 32 t. Yacht club with pool. Useful before/after the Donana area.

name: Puerto Deportivo de Chipiona
approachNotes: Chipiona lighthouse (62 m, tallest in Spain) conspicuous ~1 nm SW of entrance. HAZARD: Bajo Salmedina reef extends ~2 nm offshore - marked by an E cardinal buoy; STAY SEAWARD of it before turning E for the harbour. Fairway buoy 1.3 nm SE of lighthouse. Visitors berth outer basin to port of entrance. Tidal range 3.5-3.7 m. Often full in high season - call ahead.
nearestTown: Chipiona (5 min); full provisioning; Jerez airport ~40 km.
notes: Gateway to the Guadalquivir; departure point for the river passage to Seville. Travel lift 45 t. Salmedina reef is a serious hazard - do not cut inside the E cardinal.
orcaNotes: Approaching the Strait orca zone; rises to high south of Cadiz.
verifyNeeded: Salmedina E-cardinal position - chart cross-check essential

name: Puerto Deportivo de Rota
approachNotes: 10 nm SSE of Chipiona, 5 nm NNW of Cadiz. After rounding the 'Corrales' fish traps (to 0.5 nm offshore), give Punta de la Morena a wide berth - shallows as little as 0.7 m within 0.5 nm. Adjacent to NATO Naval Station Rota - follow marked channel; military area to S. Max draft 2.5 m limits deeper keels.
nearestTown: Rota (adjacent); excellent small-town provisioning; Jerez airport ~30 km (direct - good crew change).
notes: Most convenient marina for the town of Rota. Shallow 2.5 m draft limit is restrictive. NATO base restricted zone to S.
orcaNotes: Within the broader Strait approach orca zone; interactions reported off Cadiz/Trafalgar in season.

name: Puerto Sherry
approachNotes: Purpose-built marina 3 nm NE of Cadiz city, just N of the main channel to El Puerto de Santa Maria. Entrance channel 100 m wide, 200 m long. Bay of Cadiz tidal streams up to 2-3 kt at springs - time the approach. Well-marked.
nearestTown: El Puerto de Santa Maria ~3 km; Jerez airport ~15 km (excellent crew change); Cadiz ~5 km.
notes: Largest marina on the Bay of Cadiz; full resort facilities. Travelift 200 t (one of the largest on this coast) - excellent haulout.
orcaNotes: Strait approach orca zone; follow GTOA advisories.

name: Real Club Nautico El Puerto de Santa Maria
approachNotes: On the Rio Guadalete, 5 nm NE of Cadiz city. Access via buoyed channel between breakwaters into the river. Strong current - care berthing. Bar at river entrance; approach on tide.
nearestTown: El Puerto de Santa Maria (immediate); sherry bodegas, tapas; Jerez airport ~15 km.
notes: Traditional yacht-club marina in attractive town; strong river current at times; limited public info - call ahead.

name: Marina Puerto America (Cadiz)
approachNotes: N end of Cadiz city within the commercial port. Bay of Cadiz is well-marked deep water. Tidal streams 2-3 kt at springs in the main channel; follow port-authority directions. Small marina (max LOA 15 m). 10 t crane, slipway.
nearestTown: Cadiz city (immediate); excellent provisioning, historic city; Jerez airport ~35 km.
notes: Compact urban marina inside Cadiz commercial port; 15 m LOA limit; 10 t crane (not a haulout). For larger yachts use RCN Cadiz or Puerto Sherry.

name: Real Club Nautico de Cadiz
approachNotes: Within Cadiz Bay near the city waterfront. 160 finger berths with water/electricity. Bay of Cadiz well-marked; tidal streams 2-3 kt at springs. Fuel, ice, ramp, dry storage.
nearestTown: Cadiz city (immediate); historic centre; Jerez airport ~35 km.
notes: Founded 1905; prestigious yacht club; fuel and ramp; limited public visitor info - call ahead.

name: Puerto Deportivo Sancti Petri
approachNotes: Inside the Sancti Petri channel (Cano de Sancti Petri), S of Chiclana. Entrance width 100 m; depth 2 m at LW; interior 0-12 m variable. Conspicuous Sancti Petri Castle on islet at channel entrance is the leading mark. Approach on rising tide - bar tricky at LW.
nearestTown: Chiclana ~8 km; Conil ~15 km; Jerez airport ~45 km.
notes: Picturesque tidal-channel marina near Sancti Petri Castle ruins; good anchorage off the entrance; staging point before Barbate/Tarifa.
orcaNotes: Strait approach orca zone; Cabo Trafalgar nearby is an interaction hotspot.

name: Puerto Deportivo de Barbate
approachNotes: 36 10.8N 005 55.5W. Protected by 1300 m breakwater + floating anti-wave barrier at entrance. Barbate lighthouse (sectorized R/W). CAUTION: almadraba (tuna-trap) nets set Apr-Jun extend E-W near the breakwater E of the entrance - go well clear to seaward/W before turning in. Dredged channel 256 m. First visit: HW +/- 2h in light conditions due to shifting sand. Tarifa 20 nm SE (~2h motor).
nearestTown: Barbate (15 min walk); fish market, supermarkets; Jerez airport ~55 km; buses to Cadiz/Algeciras.
notes: THE key Strait-of-Gibraltar staging port eastbound: depart ~3h before HW Gibraltar for the favourable tidal gate. 100 transit berths; 45 t travel lift. Rest day here to wait for Poniente/light wind is wise. Almadraba hazard Apr-Jun is serious.
orcaNotes: Barbate-Tarifa is the single most active orca-interaction area (GTOA), esp. May-Aug. Follow CCS Tarifa advice: in interaction, stop the boat / hands off wheel / report on VHF 16; keep to shallow water (<20 m) along the coast where advised.

name: Puerto de Tarifa
approachNotes: Tarifa Island (connected by causeway); lighthouse at S tip conspicuous. Harbour 0.75 nm NNE of island tip. APBA manages 118 small-craft berths (5-11 m) for local/day-trip vessels. Yachts on passage: REFUGE ONLY, berth alongside central jetty if space. Heavy Tarifa-Tangier ferry traffic - give way. Levante can blow F8-9 funnelling through the Strait. Los Cabezos shoal breaks W of entrance even in calm; tunny nets off approaches esp. W.
nearestTown: Tarifa (adjacent, windsurf capital); basic provisioning; no fuel/yacht facilities; Gibraltar ~15 nm E.
notes: REFUGE/EMERGENCY ONLY for yachts - no fuel, no dedicated yacht facilities. Levante is strong and dangerous; the Strait amplifies wind 2-3 Bf. Strait E-going stream begins near HW Tarifa. Los Cabezos shoal NW is dangerous; ferry traffic heavy and fast.
orcaNotes: Epicentre of orca interactions in the Strait. CCS Tarifa coordinates advisories on VHF; check before transit.

name: Alcaidesa Marina (La Linea)
approachNotes: 36 09N 005 22W. Bay of Algeciras, W of Gibraltar. Excellent shelter all weather; deep-water access. Call VHF 09 before arrival. Keep clear of the Algeciras commercial shipping lane / TSS. La Linea border crossing to Gibraltar ~2 km (pedestrian); Gibraltar airport 2 km. Strait currents 4-7 kt at springs - time the tidal gate.
nearestTown: La Linea de la Concepcion (adjacent); Gibraltar across border ~2 km walk; Gibraltar airport 2 km (BA, easyJet) - prime crew change.
notes: Largest, best-equipped marina in the region; ideal final Spanish stop before Gibraltar/Med. 75 t hoist - best haulout on the Strait. Fuel: diesel, 95-octane, Calor gas. Spanish customs territory (clear into Gibraltar at a GIB marina).
orcaNotes: Strait orca zone; interactions common on the approach from the W.

name: Queensway Quay Marina, Gibraltar
approachNotes: E aspect of middle Gibraltar Harbour, behind the outer mole. Radio VHF 71 for clearance before entry; Gibraltar VTS monitors VHF 16/12. Q-flag required. Anchoring anywhere in Gibraltar Harbour is prohibited. Pre-arrival notification 1-2 days via HM Customs Gibraltar (ASYCUDA). Marina staff handle customs/immigration. Duty-free diesel (Cepsa). No haul-out; no LPG refills.
nearestTown: Gibraltar town (10-15 min walk); Morrisons/Eroski supermarkets; Gibraltar airport (BA, easyJet) - excellent crew-change hub; duty-free.
notes: Quieter, residential atmosphere; preferred by long-stay cruisers. Tax-free, low-cost fuel. Chandleries: Sheppard's, Gib Yachts. Gibraltar remains outside Schengen (May 2026); passports checked.
orcaNotes: Strait orca zone on the W approach.

name: Ocean Village Marina, Gibraltar
approachNotes: W side of Gibraltar, outside the N end of the harbour, adjacent to the airport runway. Pre-arrival notification required (ASYCUDA); Q-flag; VHF 71; marina staff handle customs/immigration. Atlantic-/bay-facing - more exposed to westerlies than Queensway Quay. Aircraft cross the marina approach when landing.
nearestTown: Gibraltar (adjacent to entertainment district, Casemates Square); Gibraltar airport immediate; La Linea border ~300 m.
notes: Vibrant, entertainment-focused; restaurants, casino, Sunborn hotel. Duty-free. Shares operator with Marina Bay. More exposed than Queensway Quay.
orcaNotes: Strait orca zone on the W approach.

name: Marina Bay, Gibraltar
approachNotes: N of Gibraltar Harbour between the North Mole and the airport runway. Pre-arrival notification (ASYCUDA); Q-flag; VHF 71; customs via marina staff. Shares operator with Ocean Village (combined ~250+ berths).
nearestTown: Gibraltar (walking distance); Morrisons; Gibraltar airport adjacent - best single-point crew change on the whole route.
notes: Traditional marina feel; preferred by long-term cruisers over Ocean Village's entertainment focus. Duty-free diesel (Cepsa). Gibraltar is a non-Schengen UK Overseas Territory (May 2026).
orcaNotes: Strait orca zone on the W approach.
